contaminant.name: Bronze Patina and Corrosion
contaminant.description: Bronze-patina contamination, it arises from oxidation of copper-tin alloys in bronze when exposed to moist air and pollutants, thus forming adherent green-brown layers that alter surface appearance. This patina shows earthy color and porous texture, it distinguishes from similar contaminants by adhering tightly on bronze while spreading unevenly on brass surfaces and thinly on copper, so chemical bonding resists mechanical scraping and poses key removal challenges. Laser cleaning applies precise energy pulses to ablate the layer selectively, thus restoring underlying metal without heat damage, and surface already exhibits integrity after treatment.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Lead Oxide Fume generation detected - moderate toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
